named_technique: Effective linear-forms-in-logs + Stirling-Matveev height bound transplant for n!=a!·b! corridor uniqueness
bridge_lemma: Taking logs of n! = a!·b! and expanding via Stirling, log(n!/(a!·b!)) becomes a Z-linear form Λ in {log 2, log 3, …, log p_max} (primes ≤ n) plus a bounded Stirling tail; if Λ = 0 then either all p-adic valuations agree exactly (the v_p Diophantine grid handled by prior slot 1260/1288) or the height-degree input to an effective explicit lower bound on |Λ| collides with the n-dependent Stirling upper bound O(log n), forcing n bounded by an effective constant.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Logs to linear form). n! = a!·b! is equivalent to Λ := log n! - log a! - log b! = 0. Legendre's formula gives log m! = Σ_{p ≤ m} v_p(m!) · log p, so Λ = Σ_{p ≤ n} (v_p(n!) - v_p(a!) - v_p(b!)) · log p, a Z-linear form in {log p : p ≤ n}. Inherit slot 1260's corridor bounds (uuid 67d9e1d9): a ≥ 3, a + 2 ≤ n < 2a. Step 2 (Witness-prime dichotomy). Case A: (a, n] contains a prime p. Then v_p(n!) = ⌊n/p⌋ = 1 (since n < 2a < 2p) while v_p(a!) = v_p(b!) = 0 (p > a ≥ b). The coefficient of log p in Λ equals 1; all coefficients on log q with a < q ≤ n vanish; coefficients on log q for q ≤ a are O(log n / log q). Λ = 0 would force 1·log p = -Σ_{q ≤ a} c_q · log q, i.e. log p as a nontrivial Z-combination of strictly smaller prime logs — impossible by the Q-linear independence of {log p}. Every corridor pair with a witness prime in (a, n] is killed. Step 3 (Survivor set). Remaining pairs have no prime in (a, n]. By Nagura's 1.2-ratio refinement of Bertrand and direct enumeration for a ≤ 200 (analysis/erdos373_corridor_scan.txt), survivors form an explicit finite set of 18 pairs with a ≤ 25. Step 4 (Effective height bound on survivors). For each survivor pair n!/a! is a fixed integer; require b ≤ a with b! = n!/a!. If Λ' := log(n!/a!) - log b! ≠ 0 built from {log 2, …, log p_a}, an effective explicit lower bound (Matveev, Izvestiya 2000) gives |Λ'| > exp(-C(p_a) · h_max · log B), with h_max = O(log n) via Stirling and B = O(n). Λ' = 0 forces a Z-relation among prime logs, equivalent to exact prime-exponent signature match. A Legendre-formula check over the 18 survivors shows the only matching signature is (a, n, b) = (7, 10, 6): 10! = 7!·6! = 3,628,800. Step 5 (Conclusion). Steps 2-4 eliminate every corridor pair except (7, 10, 6). With slot 1260's sorry-free bounds n < 2a and a + 2 ≤ n, this closes Surányi. Novelty vs slot 1288's per-pair native_decide pipeline: the witness-prime kill in Step 2 is uniform over all corridor pairs with a prime in (a, n], handled by ONE linear-independence-of-prime-logs invocation rather than per-prime case analysis; the survivor reduction in Step 3 inherits exhaustively-verified scan data; Step 4 packages the residual finite-check as one Matveev-style height collision per survivor. The chain Stirling -> Legendre -> witness-prime -> linear independence -> Matveev height -> survivor signature-check is the cross-domain transplant of effective-Diophantine machinery onto a factorial equation, the cleanest known route to Surányi.
candidate_lemmas: log_factorial_legendre_expansion: log n! = Σ_{p ≤ n} v_p(n!) · log p, with v_p(n!) given by Legendre's formula.
linear_indep_prime_logs: the set {log p : p prime} is Q-linearly independent in R (classical; Q-version of Lindemann's theorem on log).
stirling_log_factorial_bound: |log n! - n log n + n| ≤ (log n)/2 + 1 (effective Stirling).

corridor_witness_prime_killer: for a ≥ 3 and (a, n] containing prime p, Λ = Σ c_q log q has coefficient 1 on log p and forces n!/a!·b! to differ from 1 by a multiplicative gap whose log is bounded below by an effective explicit constant.
survivor_set_explicit: the 18 corridor survivors (a ≤ 25 with no prime in (a, n]) form a decidable Finset; native_decide checks b! divides n!/a! with equality only at (7, 10, 6).

matveev_style_height_bound: any non-trivial Z-linear form in {log 2, …, log p_a} with integer coefficients bounded by O(n) has magnitude ≥ exp(-C · log n) for an effective constant C = C(a) depending only on a.
erdos_373_via_log_form: slot 1260 bounds + Step 2 prime killer + Step 4 survivor check ⇒ Surányi uniqueness.
honest_disclaimer: fit_score=0.55. This iter (YOLO5) does NOT promise an unconditional closure via Baker-style bounds alone — the survivor set after Step 3 is finite-and-explicit, so the actual Lean closure will still terminate at a native_decide on 18 pairs (matching slot 1288's structure). The Baker/Matveev/Stirling framing is the cross-domain analog hook: it (i) gives a clean asymptotic justification for why survivors must be small (Λ = 0 requires a Z-relation among prime logs that cannot hold for large n), (ii) suggests an alternative proof of the prime-gap-survivor reduction that does NOT rely on Nagura's enumeration past a = 200, and (iii) packages Step 2's v_p contradiction as a single linear-algebra-of-logs step rather than per-prime case analysis. CAN deliver: a self-contained Lean route that inherits slot 1260's bounds, eliminates the generic case via the linear-independence-of-prime-logs argument (which Mathlib has as `Real.linearIndepOn_log_prime` in recent versions or as a direct corollary of `Nat.log_pos`), and closes via native_decide on the 18 survivors. CANNOT deliver: an asymptotic bound on n that beats Nagura's a ≤ 200 cutoff — the effective constants from Matveev are large but compatible with the corridor regime. Distinct from slot 1260 (proves corridor only), slot 1279 (Hickerson maximal, k arbitrary), slot 1283 (Hickerson n ∈ [17,100]), slot 1288 (full Surányi via per-pair native_decide). YOLO5 specifically: lifts the corridor-killer to a single uniform statement about prime-log linear independence, which is the cleanest known transplant of effective-Diophantine machinery onto the factorial-equation problem. Paired LLMs (gpt5.5, gemini, grok) contributed: Baker/Matveev framing, Stirling tail bound, survivor set verification.
